sections.title: Hi, I'm Miles
sections.subtitle: Software Engineer Intern
sections.description: Welcome to my digital portfolio! I am currently interning at A*STAR Advanced Remanufacturing and Technology Centre (ARTC) within the Digital Supply Chain (DSC) department.

blocks.content:
I am interning at A*STAR ARTC in the Digital Supply Chain (DSC) department. My primary focus has been on full-stack web development, exploring modern frameworks and rules engines to enhance supply chain workflows.

During the first eight weeks of my internship, my objective was to become familiar with the organization, development environment, and technology stack while contributing to software development projects.

As a Software Engineer Intern, my assigned job role involves setting up local development environments, contributing to the existing DWA project, and researching/developing workflow architectures.

My responsibilities included cloning repositories, configuring PostgreSQL databases, running Prisma migrations, integrating Clerk Authentication, and developing a custom rules engine prototype.

sections.title: Internship Timeline
sections.description: Chronological breakdown of key milestones, technical tasks, and deliverables completed during my internship at A*STAR ARTC.

timelineItems.title: Onboarding & Environment Setup
timelineItems.subtitle: Setting up Local Stack
timelineItems.description: Acclimatized to the organization's workflows and development standards. Set up the local environment, cloned internal repositories using SSH, configured PostgreSQL and Prisma schemas, and successfully integrated Clerk Authentication for secure local session management.

timelineItems.title: DWA Project Contribution
timelineItems.subtitle: Codebase Navigation & APIs
timelineItems.description: Collaborated with the team to understand the existing DWA (Digital Workflow Assistant) codebase. Assisted in debugging API routes, configuring databases, running schema migrations, and researching scalable workflow architectures.

timelineItems.title: Rules Engine Prototype Development
timelineItems.subtitle: System Architecture Design
timelineItems.description: Researched and built a custom rules engine prototype powered by JSON. The engine runs rule evaluations against input facts (e.g. user conditions, regional context) and traverses graph structures to decide workflow triggers dynamically.

timelineItems.title: Interactive Rules Editor & UI Polish
timelineItems.subtitle: Frontend Development & Feedback loops
timelineItems.description: Designed and iterated on an interactive front-end editor for the custom JSON rules engine. Incorporating constructive supervisor feedback, I streamlined rule creation, improved node-edge visualization, and refined the overall visual layouts.

timelineItems.title: OCR & Voice Form Filling Development
timelineItems.subtitle: AI-Driven Form Automation
timelineItems.description: Individually engineered a 'mini-agent' prototype for voice form filling using voice-to-text and intent parsing. Expanded the scope from a single intent to three (fill field, query content, check empty fields) and developed a workaround for browser-based hands-free voice activation. Gathered team feedback and recorded a demo video to validate customer demand before project integration.

timelineItems.title: Demo Video Production & Editing
timelineItems.subtitle: Product Walkthrough & Showcase
timelineItems.description: Recorded and post-processed a comprehensive video demonstration showcasing the smart Voice-to-Text form filling features in action to present to team supervisors.

timelineItems.title: Digitalising Customer Workflows
timelineItems.subtitle: Workflow Visualization & Orchestration
timelineItems.description: Helped to digitalise existing companies' workflows using our DWA (Digital Workflow Assistant) platform. Acquired hands-on experience with complicated client workflows, Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP), and SOP enforcement. Participated in customer meetings to clarify expectations and provide technical recommendations.

sections.title: GWBL1: Content and Evidences
sections.description: Tangible evidence of tasks completed, projects worked on, and challenges overcome.

items.title: Full-Stack Project Environment Setup
items.subtitle: React, Next.js, Prisma, PostgreSQL & Clerk
items.content: My first technical task involved setting up the project environment on my local machine. This included cloning repositories using Git and SSH, installing dependencies, configuring PostgreSQL, running Prisma migrations, and integrating Clerk authentication. This gave me practical experience in full-stack application setup and resolving environment issues through research.

items.title: JSON Rules Engine Implementation
items.subtitle: Rule-Based Workflow Execution
items.content: This implementation explores how a JSON rules engine can drive workflow decisions using facts, conditions, graph nodes, and labelled edges. The engine receives input facts such as age and country, evaluates them against JSON-style rules, then follows the matching path through the graph until it reaches a final action. In the example shown, the facts pass the age rule, match the country rule for CN, and finish at the BLOCK action. This helped me understand how business logic can be represented as configurable data instead of hard-coded application branches.

items.title: JSON Rules Editor Iteration
items.subtitle: UI/UX & Feedback Loops
items.content: Transitioning from the prototype, I developed a JSON Rules Editor refined through multiple iterations based on supervisor feedback. Regular reviews allowed me to monitor progress, validate requirements, and adjust efficiently, highlighting the importance of agile communication.

items.title: OCR and Voice Form Filling
items.subtitle: AI-Powered Automation
items.content: Designed and built an individual prototype of an AI-powered 'mini-agent' for smart form filling, which converts voice inputs to text and applies natural language processing (NLP) to clarify user intent. I expanded the project's scope from the team's initial idea of a single intent (filling fields) to support three distinct intents: filling form fields, querying specific field contents, and checking which fields remain empty. I also conceived and built a hands-free 'Hey Siri'-style voice activation workaround for the browser environment, allowing voice trigger commands through keyword matching without manual touch. While this was an individual task, I iterated on the design based on team feedback and compiled a demo video to showcase these capabilities to customers before committing to main codebase integration. Developing and editing this video demo was a new skill I had to learn and master from scratch during this internship.
items.image: /OCR and voice to fill up form.png

items.title: Digitalising Workflows for Partner Companies
items.subtitle: Interactive Customer Flow Builders
items.content: Helped to digitalise existing companies' workflows using our DWA (Digital Workflow Assistant) platform—the main project within our group. Through this work, I gained a deep understanding of complex business workflows, Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) compliance, and SOP enforcement. I also participated directly in client meetings, learning how to clarify customer expectations, address their extensive operational requirements, and provide professional technical suggestions and opinions.

blocks.content:
**Broadening of Technical & Media Skills:** Addressed learning gaps in React, Next.js, Prisma, and PostgreSQL through independent research. Additionally, I had to learn video editing and production from scratch during the internship to compile customer-facing product demonstrations.

**Applying Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities (KSA):** Applied software engineering principles to configure environments and evaluate rules engines. I acquired deep operational workflow knowledge, particularly in Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) and Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) compliance, which I applied to help digitalise complex processes.

**Soft Skills & Client Engagement:** Developed vital communication skills by participating directly in client meetings, learning how to present technical opinions, ask clarifying questions, and manage extensive customer requirements. I also practiced team coordination by aligning team workflows using workflow visualization tools.

**Operational & Ad-hoc Contribution:** Supported ARTC's daily operations (detailed under the **Internship Chores** tab page, separate from the main page) by coordinating technical support, managing multi-display presentation systems for events, and handling ad-hoc chores like document binding. Learning to accept constructive team feedback and pivot architectures taught me the importance of agile collaboration.

sections.title: Internship Chores
sections.description: Ad-hoc operational support, administrative assistance, and hardware management duties performed during my internship.

blocks.subtitle: IT & Hardware Operations
blocks.title: Multi-Display Presentation Systems
blocks.content: Responsible for setting up, managing, and maintaining multi-display system configurations in the presentation rooms. Provided technical support for events, seminars, and organizational presentations to ensure smooth operational delivery.

blocks.subtitle: One-Day Administration
blocks.title: Document Binding
blocks.content: Assisted with formatting, printing, organizing, and binding physical copies of technical documents and reports. This administrative task was completed as part of team operational support.

**Accomplishments:** Successfully set up full-stack projects independently, evaluated complex architecture, and shipped iterative UI improvements.

**Challenges Faced:** Navigating new tech stacks and understanding large existing codebases presented steep learning curves, which I overcame through persistence and effective communication with my supervisor.

**Learning and Growth:** The internship provides immense opportunities for learning modern stack development and professional workflow practices. Moving forward, I aspire to take on more complex architectural challenges and further hone my skills as a scalable full-stack developer.
